steps.title: Move the nonmatching term
steps.content:
Start with the given equation and move the term without $s$ to the other side:
$$21qrt-3qrs+12rst=0$$
$$-3qrs+12rst=-21qrt$$

steps.title: Factor out the target variable
steps.content:
Both terms on the left contain $s$, so factor it out:
$$s(-3qr+12rt)=-21qrt$$
Then divide by the remaining factor:
$$s=\frac{-21qrt}{-3qr+12rt}$$

steps.title: Simplify the denominator
steps.content:
Factor the denominator and cancel the common factors:
$$s=\frac{-21qrt}{-3r(q-4t)}=\frac{7qt}{q-4t}$$
The answer is B.
